bastion.reason: Huge hitbox makes charging rail energy easy; charged shots land reliably. Slide can break line of sight on Sentry mode, and his ultimate is easier to play around.
Can pressure Bastion from above or the side even when he sits deep. Sticky Bombs connect easily; copying Bastion is very strong. Do not float in Flight during Sentry—you can die with no cover.

junkrat.reason: You can ignore both front poke and side combos and attack from the air one-sidedly. Skilled Junkrat may still anti-air you.
Same idea as Echo—one-sided air damage. Skilled Junkrat may still anti-air you.
Strong vs Junkrats who flank wide—Virus plus primary can secure kills. Weak vs Junkrats who only poke from the core with their team.

mei.reason: Ice Wall is irrelevant from the air. Icicles rarely stick, and headshot one-shot risk is low even if tagged.
Same as Echo—Mei's strengths are largely nullified from the air.

soldier_76.reason: If you close with cooldowns ready you heavily win the duel; as long as you do not panic-miss shurikens you usually come out ahead.
When Soldier is dominating he often plays wide, so many 1v1s happen—stable burst from there often secures the kill.
Pick isolated Soldier from stealth. If Virus lands, commit; if not, Translocator out. Sticking to that plan works well.

symmetra.reason: From the air you ignore her beam pressure and turrets.
Good vs Symmetra who beams on the ground—larger projectiles make headshots easier. Harder vs players who use Teleporter well.

torbjorn.reason: Break the turret from the air; its shots do not hit you while you poke. Often a one-sided kill.
Same as Echo—delete the turret from above.

genji.reason: When Genji flanks, Hack plus Virus can kill before healing sticks. Engage Genji first, then others—reversing order risks getting dashed down.

reaper.reason: One-sided air damage. Sticky Bomb his Teleport landing to force early Wraith so he cannot do his job.
Higher ranks Reaper roams for supports; Cassidy peeling with support can guarantee Grenade sticks and discourage dives. Large head and hopping movement make headshots easier.

tracer.reason: Season 12 Tracer nerfs plus Torbjorn's anti-Tracer armor make this matchup workable.
Advanced. Good Genji can one-shot Tracer with blade plus primary burst. Even a failed pick, Swift Strike lets him escape—Tracer is often unfavored. Treat as reference only.

venture.reason: Venture's primary is short range so Echo barely takes return damage; after Bomb, burrow usually does not save. Echo's presence alone makes Venture passive. Watch tight rooms where Echo can collapse on you.
Grenade stick denies burrow and forces cooldowns so Venture dies reliably. Play with support so Venture cannot run you over.

echo.reason: Poking Echo feeds large-projectile Cassidy; combo kills rarely stick due to HP. Bomb is heavily reduced by Combat Roll. No scope makes tracking easier. Cassidy is the default answer.
Punish the end of Flight. If Echo plays passive, ignore and focus the front. Fighting her head-on you can die to primary damage—stay aware.

pharah.reason: The classic Pharah answer—close-range Sticky Bomb into beam chip is the baseline. Do not force the duel if she plays very high or only long-range poke.

sombra.reason: Grenade keeps her out of melee; stable burst into Teleport. Easier to peel when she dives your support.
Turret limits her pathing; she wastes time hacking or destroying it while it still deals damage.
Hard counter—meeting in stealth is hopeless. Hack and Virus get cleansed; EMP loses to Protection Suzu.
You must burn Recall first or you cannot operate. Generally mirror Tracer and track her first.
No real duel win condition; even a stealth opener you get shredded by gun damage.
Hardest tank—Teleporting back to team often dies to punch through Ravenous Vortex. Even when hacked he is too durable for much value.

ashe.reason: Ashe's strafe keeps the head line stable; at range Ashe cannot establish sightlines. Widow often forces an Ashe swap. Mid-to-close Ashe combos kill you—manage distance and back line.

cassidy.reason: With Season 12 Ashe, outside Grenade range you are favored at every band. Cassidy is usually answered by the whole comp; Ashe is the strongest individual pick.

hanzo.reason: Up close Genji is favored; Hanzo cannot peel a Genji fighting elsewhere.
Strong vs Hanzo, but at mid-close his large projectiles can body you. Fight mid-far or break LOS and burst in.

sojourn.reason: Sojourn with no rail charge dies to Genji—only Disruptor Shot or Slide to escape.
Same broad idea as Genji, but Echo can farm your energy while you focus others. Break LOS, then close with abilities.

widowmaker.reason: Stealth in with Virus plus headshot burst. If the rest of your roster loses teamfights, swap off Sombra and play frontline.
Approach is harder than Sombra, but Tracer is far stronger into everyone except Widow.
Situational for high-ground maps or when they run double sniper.

dva.reason: Matrix soaks shots while cutting angles; Power Slide is not Matrixable.
High HP survives Boosters plus Micro Missiles; primary spray pierces Matrix.
Beam pierces the mech, but at higher skill brackets the matchup is harder to rely on—mechanics matter.

doomfist.reason: Play with support; Grenade as block ends. High HP resists one-shots—watch punch stopping Fortify timing.
Hack during punch or slam block. Do not tunnel only Doom—play the whole map.

winston.reason: Breaking bubble saves the team. Hold high ground near support for ambushes; do not show early or jump angle gets read.
Move with support; Fan the Hammer breaks bubble; Deadeye answers Primal Rage.

wrecking_ball.reason: Hack the rollout to support; bait the engage rather than chasing blindly.
Grenade before Piledriver; you can also aim the landing. Roll through Minefield plus Pile combos.

mauga.reason: Headshots spin the chainguns; Slide the charge.
Self-sustain comp—damage Mauga for ult tempo; copying Mauga is very strong.
Self-sustain; huge hitbox means high damage. Cage windows can enable Death Blossom.

orisa.reason: Headshots farm rail energy; Slide the ultimate.
Her kit is passive—slow chip from the sky works.

roadhog.reason: Headshots spin rail; Slide while he walks forward in the open.
No armor so shotguns shred; 300 HP often survives Chain Hook.
Hook does not reach—stable damage from range.

zarya.reason: Range and mobility keep her primary from connecting; bubbles are awkward to peel with.
Burst damage forces the whole team to respect angles.
Split after bubble use; easier headshots; wall counters Graviton Surge; favorable ult timing.
Self-sustain comps only—keep headshotting; Deadeye answers Graviton Surge.

junker_queen.reason: Self-peel comp—split to bait shout; wall stops Rampage; freeze beats axe swing.
Ignore Queen or bait shout—both are viable.
Self-peel on indoor maps only—beginner-friendly option.

ramattra.reason: Air damage avoids eating Ram's kit—focus others or punish after Ravenous Vortex.
Front and side pressure for ult tempo; Ramattra copy is strong.

reinhardt.reason: Fire Strike or ignore—Charge and Earthshatter are less scary.
Same as Pharah—Charge and Shatter are less scary.
Large magazine keeps pressure up; Power Slide dodges Charge; Disruptor pierces barrier.
Situational—sit on your tank and provide support fire.

sigma.reason: Self-peel—split Sigma to burn abilities; primary spray pierces Kinetic Grasp.
Break barrier from the front, then look for Sticky Bombs under half HP; Sigma copy is strong. Do not overextend for copy past the barrier.
Works in poke wars—a flank hack deletes barrier for a pick. At higher ranks flankers often deny this value.
